name: Southwest Bread"gilroy
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Oil Packed Sun Dried Tomato (drained and chopped)
1 tablespoon Jalapeno Pepper, seeded and minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first three ingredients in a large bowl; stir.
2. Add water, yogurt, and honey; stir.
3. Let stand for 30 minutes.
4. Add beans and next 12 ingredients (through vinegar); stir.
5. Stir in 4 1/2 cups bread flour to form a soft dough.
6. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface.
7. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 8 minutes), adding enough of the remaining flour, one tablespoon at a time, to prevent the dough from sticking to hands.
8. Place dough in a large bowl coated with cooking spray, turning to coat the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, for 45 minutes or until doubled in bulk.
9. Punch dough down. Divide dough into 4 equal portions, and shape each portion into a 6-inch round loaf.
10. Place loaves 4 inches apart on large baking sheets coated with cooking spray.
11. Make 4 (1/8-inch-deep) diagonal cuts across the tops of loaves.
12. Dust loaves with flour. Cover and let rise for another 45 minutes or until doubled in bulk.
13. Bake at 375°F for 25 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped; let cool on wire racks.
